mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses below 9.0 percent, it signals a confirmed bottom in the labor market, triggering a shift in investor risk appetite toward cyclical equities and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative or flat 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances over a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in housing starts and consumer credit extension confirms broad-based demand recovery, which lifts corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by at least 1.5% over any 3-year rolling window, indicating the proxies do not capture incremental demand information.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and operational efficiency, which directly improves corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns within a one-week trading window.
failure_condition: Monthly rank correlation between INDPRO changes and 5-day forward SPX returns remains below 0.05 across all post-2000 data, proving no predictive link exists.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.0 percent confirms an accelerating economic expansion, which forces equity analysts to raise earnings per share estimates and pushes S&P 500 prices higher over the following month.
failure_condition: Forward returns following GDP growth spikes above 2.0% are statistically indistinguishable from zero (95% confidence interval includes zero) over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid payroll growth combined with stable consumer prices eliminates stagflation fears, allowing the market to price in higher corporate profits without discount rate penalties, thereby driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term in a multivariate regression yields a coefficient with a p-value greater than 0.10, demonstrating no joint predictive power for 60-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: Continuous month-over-month increases in consumer credit extension reflect sustained household spending momentum, which directly supports retail and consumer discretionary earnings and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The signal generates negative forward returns in more than 60% of observations where RSAFS rises consecutively for three months, invalidating the momentum premise.

mechanism_narrative: A falling federal funds rate paired with declining consumer prices removes the dual drag of higher discount rates and shrinking nominal revenues, creating a highly favorable environment for equity valuation expansion over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce a positive Sharpe ratio relative to the S&P 500 benchmark over any 5-year rolling period, indicating the combination does not enhance risk-adjusted returns.
